description: A Go-Problems-style tactical puzzle mode plus automatic tracking of interesting positions from lost games. Bridges the gap between Learn (tutorial) and Play (full game).

practice_mode.description: Short tactical problems. Quick in, quick out. Solve a few at the café in 5 minutes.
practice_mode.entry_point: Main menu button: 'Practice' — sits between 'Learn' and 'Play'

difficulty_levels.description: One-move problems. Find the capture, find the check, find the escape.
Two-move problems. Find the tactic that wins material or forces checkmate.
concepts: Horse fork (attack two pieces at once)

difficulty_levels.description: Three to four move combinations. Requires seeing the whole sequence.
concepts: Chariot sacrifice leading to Cannon mate
Clearance sacrifice (move a piece to open a line)
Flying General as the finishing move

puzzle_data_format.description: Each puzzle is a JSON object that can be loaded from a curated set or generated from saved game positions.

schema.fen: string — board position in Cờ tướng FEN format

solution.player_move: string — algebraic notation of correct move
solution.opponent_response: string — forced or best opponent reply
alternative_correct: string — other acceptable moves if any

schema.hint: string — text hint shown when player requests help (e.g., 'Look at the file between the Generals')
schema.source_game_id: string|null — if generated from a loss, link back to the game

puzzle_screen.layout: Same board component as Play mode — no new rendering needed
puzzle_screen.prompt: Bottom sheet shows the objective (e.g., 'Checkmate in 2 — Red to move')
puzzle_screen.interaction: Standard tap-to-select, tap-to-move from Play mode
puzzle_screen.on_correct_move: Board plays opponent's forced response automatically (200ms delay), then waits for next player move
puzzle_screen.on_wrong_move: Piece snaps back, bottom sheet shows 'Not quite — try again' with subtle shake animation
puzzle_screen.on_solve: Confetti or green flash, 'Solved!' message, auto-advance to next puzzle after 1.5s
puzzle_screen.on_give_up: 'Show solution' button appears after 2 failed attempts (easy/medium) or 3 failed attempts (hard)

session_flow.selection: Player picks difficulty, puzzles served sequentially
session_flow.mixed_mode: Optional — shuffles across difficulties, weighted by spaced repetition box
session_flow.end_of_session: Summary: solved count, average attempts, streak

navigation.back_button: Returns to Practice menu, progress auto-saved
navigation.skip_button: Skip puzzle without penalty (doesn't count as failed)
navigation.hint_button: Shows hint text from puzzle data, deducts from hints_available count

initial_puzzle_set.description: Ship with a curated starter set. Expand from game losses over time.
initial_puzzle_set.source: Curated from classic Cờ tướng tactical patterns. JSON file bundled with app.
categories_to_cover: Cannon screen checkmate (the most common Cờ tướng tactic)
Sacrifice to clear a file or diagonal

progress_tracking.storage: localStorage with Safari private browsing fallback

per_puzzle.best_attempt: number — fewest wrong moves before solving

aggregate.current_streak: number — consecutive puzzles solved first try

spaced_repetition.description: Same Leitner box system from lesson plan. Failed puzzles return to Box 1, solved puzzles advance.
spaced_repetition.integration: Shared system with Learn mode puzzles — all puzzles live in the same Leitner tracker

loss_tracker.description: Automatically saves interesting positions from lost games to build a personalized puzzle library.

trigger.on_checkmate: Save position from 3 moves before checkmate
trigger.on_significant_material_loss: Save position when player loses a Chariot, Cannon, or Horse without compensation (optional, can enable later)

what_to_save.fen: Board state from the critical moment (not the final position)
what_to_save.logic: Walk back the move history 3 half-moves from the end position. That's typically where the mistake happened.
what_to_save.full_game_fen_history: Also save the complete move history for optional full replay

saved_position_schema.fen: string — position at the critical moment
saved_position_schema.turn: string — whose turn at the saved position
saved_position_schema.reviewed: boolean — has the player replayed this position
saved_position_schema.converted_to_puzzle: boolean — has this been turned into a practice puzzle
saved_position_schema.player_notes: string|null — optional note the player can add (e.g., 'missed the cannon screen')

storage.overflow: When limit reached, remove oldest reviewed positions first

post_game_prompt.message: Bottom sheet slides up: 'Want to see where it went wrong?'

options.label: Review
options.action: Load the saved position in review mode

options.label: Later
options.action: Dismiss, position is saved to loss library for later

loss_library.display: List of saved positions with date, AI difficulty, and review status
loss_library.thumbnail: Small board preview showing the position
loss_library.sort: Most recent first, unreviewed positions highlighted

review_mode.description: Replay from the critical moment. Try a different move and see what happens.

ui_flow.board: Standard board loaded with the saved FEN position
ui_flow.prompt: Bottom sheet: 'This is where things went wrong. Red to move — try a different approach.'
ui_flow.interaction: Player makes moves, AI responds in real time (using same difficulty as original game)
features: Undo available to try different lines
No move limit — play it out as far as you want
If player finds checkmate: 'Nice recovery! Save as puzzle?' option appears

convert_to_puzzle.description: If the position has a clean tactical solution, player or app can convert it to a practice puzzle.
convert_to_puzzle.manual: Player taps 'Save as puzzle' after finding a good line
convert_to_puzzle.auto_detection: If position has a forced checkmate in 1-3 moves (detected by minimax), suggest conversion automatically

on_convert.action: Create a puzzle entry in the practice puzzle library
on_convert.source_game_id: Links back to the original loss
on_convert.difficulty: Auto-assigned based on solution length: 1 move = easy, 2 = medium, 3+ = hard

menu_structure.description: Updated main menu with Practice as the bridge between Learn and Play

sub_menu.label: My Mistakes
sub_menu.description: Positions from your losses — review and learn from them

dependencies.game_engine: DONE — all piece movement and rules
dependencies.ai_minimax: DONE — used for opponent responses in puzzles and solution verification
dependencies.fen_snapshots: DONE — used for saving and loading positions
dependencies.board_rendering: DONE — same component for puzzles, review, and play
dependencies.move_history: DONE — used for walking back 3 moves on loss

new_code_needed: Puzzle data loader — parse puzzle JSON and serve to board
Puzzle interaction handler — validate player moves against solution tree
Loss capture hook — trigger on game end, walk back move history, save FEN
Loss library UI — list view with board thumbnails
Review mode — load FEN, allow free play against AI from that position
Puzzle-from-loss converter — create puzzle entry from reviewed position
Practice progress tracker — Leitner box state in localStorage
Curated puzzle set — 45 puzzles in JSON (20 easy, 15 medium, 10 hard)
implementation_notes.estimated_effort: 1 week for puzzle mode + loss tracker. Curated puzzle content is the most time-consuming part — may want AI assistance generating the initial 45 puzzles from known Cờ tướng tactical patterns.
